chapter_context.summary: A Judean man of God prophesies against the altar at Bethel, but is later deceived by an old northern prophet into disobeying God's command; he is slain by a lion for his disobedience.

outline.text: A Man of God out of Judah Prophesies Against Bethel's Altar
outline.explanation: This section focuses on A Man of God out of Judah Prophesies Against Bethel's Altar (13:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: An Old Prophet of Bethel Deceives the Judean Man of God
outline.explanation: This section focuses on An Old Prophet of Bethel Deceives the Judean Man of God (13:11-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Disobedient Prophet Slain by a Lion; Buried by the Old Prophet
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Disobedient Prophet Slain by a Lion; Buried by the Old Prophet (13:23-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Jeroboam Persists in His Evil Priestly Ordinations
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeroboam Persists in His Evil Priestly Ordinations (13:33-34), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 13, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
